scenarios.title: renders Hello Shadow DOM text inside shadow root @positive
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to the shadow DOM demo page and locate the paragraph inside the #content host element's shadow root.
expectedAssertions: paragraph inside #content shadow root contains 'Hello Shadow DOM'

locatorTable.notes: Mechanical By.id("x") → locator('#x') per KB-6 Rule 1. No aria evidence to promote to getByRole. Reviewer: confirm element type (see Q5).
Playwright CSS engine auto-pierces open shadow DOM. No explicit shadow root access needed. If shadow root is closed-mode, this translation fails — see Q2.

CSS tag p inside shadow root. Single

assumed (see Q1). Reviewer alternative: page.locator('#content').getByText('Hello Shadow DOM') if text is unique on page (elevates to tier-3). See pin 1.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment:
Q1 unresolved: multiple

in shadow root possible — .first() index is fragile
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback:
Inspect live page at https://bonigarcia.dev/selenium-webdriver-java/shadow-dom.html; if text is unique use page.getByText('Hello Shadow DOM'); if multiple

exist add .first() with the WHY-comment above.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q unresolved: toContainText (substring) mirrors AssertJ .contains() — use toHaveText if exact match is required
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If paragraph text is exactly 'Hello Shadow DOM' with no surrounding whitespace, switch to toHaveText('Hello Shadow DOM').
